title: Rhysati Ynr
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=GFFA |image=[[File:Rhysati.jpg]] |name=Rhysati Ynr |homeworld=[[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]'' |birth= |death= |species=[[Human/Legends|Human]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=new |height=1.6 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |mass= |hair=[[Color/Legends|Blonde]] |feathers= |eyes=Hazel |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *[[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] **[[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Today we're all pilots, we're all on the same side—and the man who beat the Redemption scenario is about to make good on all those dinner and drink promises he made to talk his wingmates into helping him win.|Rhysati Ynr|}} '''Rhysati Ynr''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] who served as a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] in the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] legendary [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] forced her family to flee their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], but she later joined the Rogues when [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] rebuilt the [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] in [[6 ABY/Legends|6.5 ABY]]. Her goal was to become the best pilot in the elite unit, and she led the rebuilt Rogues' [[Two Flight]] during their first official [[Battle in the Chorax system|mission]]. Ynr [[Starfighter combat/Legends|participated]] in many key [[Space warfare/Legends|battles]], including the [[Second Battle of Borleias|capture]] of [[Borleias/Legends|Borleias]], and she and her fellow Rogues went undercover to help the New Republic [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|liberate]] the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] from the Empire. Ynr was [[Mission above Imperial City|flying]] her [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing starfighter]] over Coruscant in [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]] when deposed Imperial leader [[Ysanne Isard]] escaped the planet in the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Lusankya/Legends|''Lusankya'']]. She and the rest of the squadron soon afterward resigned their commissions and [[Antilles's Rogues|independently]] pursued the [[Bacta War]] against Isard, which culminated in the successful [[Battle of Thyferra]]. Rogue Squadron were welcomed back into the New Republic with open [[Limb/Legends|arms]], but Ynr left active service in order to start a family with fellow Rogue [[Nawara Ven]], with whom she was romantically involved. The two soon [[Marriage/Legends|married]], and in [[43 ABY]], Ynr was recruited by [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] to fly an [[Eta-5 interceptor|interceptor]] in a [[Mission to save Kessel|mission]] to save [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] from a series of [[Groundquake/Legends|groundquakes]] that threatened to destroy the entire [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. ==Biography== ===Going Rogue=== {{Quote|The quest for justice has brought us all to the Alliance, has it not? Rhys, you want justice for the dislocation of your family when the Empire made them flee Bespin.|Nawara Ven|}} Rhysati Ynr was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] forced her family to flee their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], but she found a new home with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. As a naturally gifted [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]], there was no question that she would make it into the legendary [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|unit]] known as [[Rogue Squadron]], and she was recruited into its ranks by [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] when he rebuilt the Rogues from the ground up in [[6 ABY/Legends|6.5 ABY]].[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] Her goal was to spend her [[Time/Legends|time]] becoming the best pilot in the squadron. Ynr was very social with her fellow Rogues, including [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn]], [[Ooryl Qrygg]], and [[Nawara Ven]]. Although she made it clear that romance was not high on her priority list, at some point she began a relationship with Ven. As the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] continued to fight the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civl War]] against the Empire, Rogue Squadron were intensely trained by Antilles over one [[Standard month/Legends|month]] at [[Folor Base]]. After they were officially activated by [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gial Ackbar/Legends|Ackbar]], Ynr served under the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] [[Rogue Seven/Legends|Rogue Seven]]. She led [[Two Flight]] on their first official mission to the [[Morobe system]], but the mission turned into a [[Battle in the Chorax system|battle]] when they were pulled out of [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] by the Imperial [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Interdictor cruiser]] [[Corusca Rainbow|''Black Asp'']]. [[File:HunterSquadron-TERC.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Ynr and her fellows fly Z-95 Headhunters to help take down Coruscant's planetary shields.]] The [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]] fled after the Rogues dueled and destroyed several of its [[TIE/IN interceptor/Legends|TIE interceptors]], and the squadron next saw [[Rout of Hensara|action]] in the [[Hensara system/Legends|Hensara system]]. There, they rescued the crew of the New Republic [[Imperial Customs Frigate|frigate]] [[Battle of Yavin (starship)|''Battle of Yavin'']], with [[One Flight|One]] and Two Flights taking out [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial ground forces]] inside [[Hensara III]]'s [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]]. Ynr's fellow Rogue [[Lujayne Forge]] was soon afterward [[Death/Legends|killed]] in her [[Sleep/Legends|sleep]] when [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] [[Raid on Talasea|raided]] their [[Talasea base|base]] on [[Talasea]], and Ynr joined her fellows at Forge's [[Funeral/Legends|memorial service]] aboard the [[Hospital ship/Legends|medical frigate]] ''[[Reprieve]]''. When Horn was confined to quarters following his brash actions in a [[Defense of Vladet|strike]] at [[Vladet]], Ynr, Ven, and fellow Rogue [[Erisi Dlarit]] paid him a visit. Ynr was personally grateful that his theatrics had [[Starfighter combat/Legends|taken out]] the [[Lancer-class frigate/Legends|''Lancer''-class frigate]] [[Ravager (Lancer-class)|''Ravager'']], which otherwise could have picked off a number of the Rogues' [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]]. The Rogues later [[Second Battle of Borleias|captured]] [[Borleias/Legends|Borleias]] from the Empire in a second strike after a disastrous [[First Battle of Borleias|first attempt]], with Ynr flying one of the only six [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] available to them for the risky [[Space warfare/Legends|attack]]. She helped destroy enemy [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] while Antilles and Horn blew up one of the [[Alderaan Biotics facility|Imperial base's]] power conduits. When Rogue Squadron pursued the ''[[Vengeance Derra IV]]''—a [[Dwarf Star-class freighter|''Dwarf Star''-class freighter]] belonging to the [[Zsinj's Empire|forces]] of the Imperial [[Warlord/Legends|Warlord]] [[Zsinj]]—to [[Mrisst/Legends|Mrisst]], Ynr flew with Dlarit and Horn under [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Aril Nunb]] in One Flight. The four of them [[Battle of Mrisst (6 ABY)|took care of]] TIE fighters and [[Interceptor/Legends|interceptors]] while Two Flight used [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] to take down ''Vengeance Derra IV''{{'s}} [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]]. Rogue Squadron were later inserted into Imperial-held [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to bring down its [[Planetary shield/Legends|planetary shields]], but only after flying to [[Kessel]] to [[Mission to Kessel (Galactic Civil War)|release]] a group of [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]] to aid them in their mission. Most of the Rogues flew cover for a landing zone on the planet while Antilles, Horn, and Ven negotiated the prisoners' release.''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]'' In Coruscant's [[Azure Dianoga Cantina]], Ynr nearly got the undercover Rogues in trouble with [[Trooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] when she refused to take lightly one trooper's [[Speciesism|xenophobia]] toward the [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] Ven, but the group was saved by a sudden communication the trooper received in his [[Helmet/Legends|helmet]]. The squadron ultimately used an [[Orbital Solar Energy Transfer Satellite|orbital mirror]] [[SCC Number Four|control center]] to create a [[Lightning (phenomenon)|lightning]] storm which brought down the shields, while Ynr, Horn, Dlarit, fellow Rogue [[Pash Cracken]], and their allies [[Inyri Forge]] and [[Asyr Sei'lar]] [[Hunter Squadron (New Republic)|flew cover]] in [[Z-95 Headhunter/Legends|Z-95 Headhunters]]. The Imperial [[Capital/Legends|capital]] went on to be [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|conquered]] by New Republic [[New Republic Defense Force/Legends|forces]] with minimal resistance, but Horn was feared dead, with the Rogues' [[Executive officer/Legends|Executive officer]], [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|Tycho Celchu]], as a murder suspect. ===Going rogue=== {{Quote|Confirm thirty-six TIEs, Three.''"
"''Confirm thirty-six, Lead, eyeballs and squints. They're coming this way and there's something else out there. My sensors aren’t picking it up at all well.|Wedge Antilles and Rhysati Ynr discover the ''Lusankya'' emerging from Coruscant's cityscape|''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]''}} It was early [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]], and the New Republic now controlled the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] capital, but the high-profile [[trial of Tycho Celchu]] left the Rogues feeling like they had little to celebrate. The deadly [[Krytos virus]] also began to ravage every [[Alien/Legends|non-human]] [[Species/Legends|species]] on the planet. Rogue Squadron were thus tasked with [[Raid on Yag-prime|stealing]] some [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]], a healing substance, from Warlord Zsinj at the [[Yag-prime]] [[Space station/Legends|space station]]. They launched alongside the [[BTL-A4 Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wings]] of [[Defender Wing]], with Ynr now flying as [[Rogue Three/Legends|Rogue Three]]. Her and Dlarit's role in the battle was to lead enemy TIEs away from the station, where Antilles and new Rogue Seilar could pick them off. The bacta was captured with ease. The Rogues' next mission was to [[Ryloth/Legends|Ryloth]] to obtain the [[Spice/Legends|spice]] [[ryll kor]], following the [[New Republic Provisional Council]]'s discovery that it could be used to help combat the virus's effects. Ryll Kor and bacta were later synthesized into [[rylca]], a bona fide cure for the Krytos virus, but only after Ynr and the Rogues failed to obtain more bacta in the [[Alderaan system/Legends|Alderaan system]]—Zsinj's [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Iron Fist (Executor-class)|''Iron Fist'']] beat them there and [[Ambush at Alderaan|destroyed]] the bacta-carrying [[Transport/Legends|transports]] of the [[Xucphra Corporation]], to Ynr and her teammates' horror. Rogue Squadron's consolation prize was the [[Strike-class medium cruiser|cruiser]] ''[[Termagant]]'', left behind by Zsinj. [[File:Lusankya_escaping_Coruscant.jpg|thumb|right|250px|''Lusankya'' escapes from Coruscant.]] When the New Republic was tipped off about a planned attack on a [[bacta storage facility]] from the [[Palpatine Counter-Insurgency Front]], a [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] cell operating on Coruscant, Rogue Squadron [[Mission above Imperial City|mobilized]] to fly cover for a [[Commando/Legends|commando]] force. Most of the Rogues stayed low over [[Galactic City/Legends|Imperial City]] while Ynr and Dlarit flew up to the level of floating [[Skyhook/Legends|skyhooks]] to cut off any potential enemy escape. The terrorists' [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeders]] and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]] were made short work of, but in the confusion, the Super [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Lusankya/Legends|''Lusankya'']] suddenly emerged from Coruscant's [[City/Legends|cityscape]]. As it turned out, Imperial ruler [[Ysanne Isard]] had fled under the planet's surface instead of offworld when the New Republic had arrived. Corran Horn was also alive and had been [[Prison/Legends|held]] in the ''Lusankya''{{'s}} [[Lusankya (prison)|namesake prison]], but the Rogue managed to escape before Isard's [[Starship/Legends|ship]] blasted out of its hiding place. He burst into Celchu's trial to declare the man innocent of his murder while meanwhile, Ynr and the rest of the Rogues watched Isard and her massive Star [[Destroyer/Legends|Destroyer]] escape the New Republic's clutches. Ynr's [[Wingmate/Legends|wingmate]] Erisi Dlarit also joined Isard in her flight, revealing herself to be a [[Spy/Legends|spy]] in Rogue Squadron's ranks. Minus the double [[Agent/Legends|agent]], the Rogues flew various pursuit and cover missions against remaining Palpatine Counter-Insurgency Front cells over the next several [[Standard day/Legends|days]]. Despite the ''Lusankya''{{'s}} getaway, a ceremony was held to welcome back Horn and to publicly exonerate Celchu. New Republic [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Mon Mothma/Legends|Mon Mothma]] publicly presented all of Rogue Squadron with the [[Coruscant Star of Valor]], a newly created award intended to be the military's highest possible honor. At the ceremony, the pilots were less than impressed when they learned that the New Republic would not pursue Isard to her new power base on [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]], a major bacta-production center, as it did not want to interfere with a planet's internal [[Politics/Legends|politics]]. They promptly resigned their commissions, with Ynr eagerly speaking for both herself and Ven when she added her name to the hat. ===Going out with a bang=== {{Quote|This isn't the armed forces, Wedge. It's more like a heavily armed bachelor party.|Ynr to Wedge Antilles in 43 ABY|''[[Fate of the Jedi: Outcast]]''}} The now-independent group of [[Antilles's Rogues]] received help from various allies and adoring private citizens, and they operated out of the Yag-prime space station as they pursued the [[Bacta War]] against Isard. Their first maneuvers were two [[Raid on Thyferran Convoy Delta-Two-Niner|separate]] [[Ambush of the bacta tankers|raids]] on [[Thyferran Convoy Delta-Two-Niner|bacta]] [[Convoy/Legends|convoys]] from her, first in the [[Chorax system/Legends|Chorax system]] and next in a [[Unidentified star system (red dwarf)|system]] with a [[Red dwarf/Legends|red dwarf]]. In the latter engagement, Ynr and Inyri Forge—now flying as Rogues [[Rogue Five/Legends|Five]] and [[Rogue Six/Legends|Six]] respectively—escorted the [[Bacta Tanker]] ''[[Xucphra Meander]]'' to Coruscant after it surrendered to the combined forces of Rogue Squadron and twelve Twi'lek [[Chir'daki]] [[Snubfighter/Legends|fighters]], the latest converts to Antilles's cause.''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]'' Isard responded to the raids by [[Battle of Halanit|destroying]] a colony on [[Halanit]] to which the Rogues had diverted some of her bacta, forcing a change in the squadron's tactics. After [[Battle of Qretu 5|striking]] bacta-producing facilities on [[Qretu 5]], they launched a series of hit-and-fade attacks on the [[Bacta Cartel/Legends|Bacta Cartel]]. Whenever they were on Yag-prime, Ynr and Ven spent most of their time together, but in the field, the Rogues narrowly survived an [[Second ambush in the Graveyard|ambush]] by Isard's forces in the [[The Graveyard|Alderaan Graveyard]]. Ynr was later called on to join a mission to [[Elshandruu Pica]] where the Rogues convinced [[Sair Yonka]], Captain of Isard's [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer|Star Destroyer]] [[Freedom|''Avarice'']], to defect to their cause. [[File:Bacta_War.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Rogue Squadron win the Bacta War.]] Alongside Antilles, Horn, [[Gavin Darklighter|Darklighter]], Qrygg, and Qrygg's [[Gand/Legends|Gand]] [[Ruetsavii|ally]] [[Vviir Wiamdi]], Ynr made her way to Yonka's cottage near the [[Picavil]] [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]]. While she and Horn secured one side of the building, Antilles and Darklighter entered through the back and laid out their proposal to their open-eared target. Isard, enraged by the turncoat, initiated a program to round up and [[Speciecide|execute]] thirty thousand of Thyferra's native [[Vratix/Legends|Vratix]], whose [[Ashern]] commandos were actively resisting her regime. Antilles knew it was time for an endgame, and the first step of his plan was to reveal to Isard that Yag-prime was the Rogues' secret base. To accomplish this, he sent Ynr, Horn, Darklighter, and Forge, along with their ally [[Mirax Terrik Horn|Mirax Terrik]] in her ''[[Pulsar Skate]]'', out to escort to the station an [[Weapon/Legends|arms]] delivery from the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Talon Karrde]]. A [[Melina Carniss|spy]] in Karrde's [[Car'das/Legends|organization]] passed the information on to Isard. Ynr and the other Rogues [[Life-form scanner/Legends|scanned]] Karrde's convoy for life-forms to ensure there were no hidden Imperial [[Troop/Legends|troops]], and they then provided their escort. Antilles later gathered all of his pilots for a briefing and told them to expect the imminent arrival of Isard's forces. Antilles's forces abandoned Yag-prime as soon as Isard's ''Lusankya'' and [[Errant Venture/Legends|''Virulence'']] showed up, and they were taken to Thyferra aboard the ''Avarice''. Ynr and the rest of the pilots [[Battle of Thyferra|dogfought]] the TIE fighters of the [[Thyferran Home Defense Corps]] while Antilles's [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] took on the ''Lusankya'', which had left its own TIE complement at Yag-prime alongside the [[Battle of Yag'Dhul (Bacta War)|captured]] ''Virulence''. Between waves of enemy fighters, the Rogues joined the attack runs on the ''Lusankya'', hammering it with proton [[Torpedo/Legends|torpedoes]] and [[Concussion missile/Legends|concussion missiles]]—including ones launched from their [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] that were [[Slave circuit|slaved]] to the X-wings' telemetry. The battle was won when the captured ''Virulence'' arrived carrying three [[Ace Squadron (New Republic)|squadrons]] of Pash Cracken's [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor/Legends|A-wings]], and both Isard and Erisi Dlarit were believed killed while trying to flee the planet. Aboard the ''Lusankya'', Antilles asked his pilots if they wanted to return to the New Republic, and Ynr was quick to agree. Ven had lost the use of his [[Limb/Legends|leg]] during the battle, however, and he decided to take over as the Rogues' new Executive Officer, rather than continue to fly an X-wing. When Rogue Squadron returned to Coruscant, their twelve X-wings were met in the sky by another twelve, led by Rogue veteran [[Derek Klivian/Legends|Derek "Hobbie" Klivian]] and claiming to be the real Rogue Squadron.''[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron]]'' As Klivian's pilots escorted Antilles's Rogues down to [[Senate Plaza/Legends|Imperial Plaza]], both squadrons engaged in aerial maneuvers, attempting to outdo each other and dazzling spectators both below and watching live on the [[HoloNet/Legends|HoloNet]]. Upon disembarking, the Rogues were met with confetti, cheerful New Republic [[Diplomat/Legends|diplomats]], and roars of appreciation from the crowd. They were ushered onto a stage as [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] of the Provisional Council gave a speech welcoming them back, and the pilots learned that their resignations had been "creatively misfiled"—the Bacta War was, retroactively, being officially sanctioned by [[New Republic High Command/Legends|New Republic High Command]]. Klivian's [[ersatz Rogue Squadron]], meanwhile, had been put together to be a visible group of Rogues for the purposes of morale. [[File:NawaraVen-JP.jpg|thumb|right|225PX|Nawara Ven, Ynr's husband]] Rogues old and new had a raucous party that night, and immediately thereafter, Rogue Squadron were assigned to the next big New Republic campaign: the [[Hunt for Zsinj]]. Several months into the campaign, Ynr was no longer flying as a Rogue,''[[X-Wing: Solo Command]]'' and by [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], she had [[Marriage/Legends|married]] Ven and resigned from the squadron in order to start a family. That [[Standard year/Legends|year]], she and Ven attended a party for the Rogues that Admiral Ackbar organized when they returned to Coruscant after the [[Battle of Bilbringi (9 ABY)|end]] of the [[Thrawn campaign]]. The party was interrupted by the arrival of [[Urlor Sette]], a prisoner from ''Lusankya'' who had been dispatched to send a taunting message to Corran Horn.''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' In the year [[43 ABY]], Ynr was summoned to Kessel by [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], who owned [[Glitterstim/Legends|glitterstim]] [[Mining/Legends|mines]] on the rocky planet. They joined a bevy of other retired pilots at the [[Calrissian-Nunb Mines]] to hear out the plan of action: navigate the mines in their starfighters and places [[Bomb/Legends|explosives]] throughout the [[Cave/Legends|caves]], which, when detonated, would halt a series of [[Groundquake/Legends|groundquakes]] that were threatening to destroy the entire planet. Ynr flew an [[Eta-5 interceptor]] during the [[Mission to save Kessel|operation]], which was armed with [[Missile/Legends|missiles]] by Inyri Forge's great-nephew [[Drathan Forge|Drathan]]. Mid-mission, while her interceptor was being reloaded, Ynr chatted with Antilles and offered to swap fighters with the interested [[General/Legends|general]]. He eagerly agreed, and Ynr took over flying his X-wing. The operation was a success, and two days later, its participants gathered in the mines' main cafeteria to celebrate. ==Behind the scenes== Rhysati Ynr was created by [[Michael A. Stackpole]] and first appeared in his [[1996]] novel ''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]''. She played a prominent role throughout the first four entries in the [[Star Wars: X-Wing (novel series)|''X-Wing'' series]], but she was rarely mentioned in later ''[[Star Wars]]'' reference books that spotlighted the Rogues' adventures. Notably, she was not featured in the [[Star Wars Insider 159|''Star Wars Insider'' 59]] article ''[[Who's Who in Rogue Squadron]]'', published in [[2002]], and the only official picture of her comes from the Japanese edition of the ''Rogue Squadron'' novel. ==Appearances== *''[[X-Wing: Rogue Squadron]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Rogue Squadron (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble (unabridged audiobook)}} *''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: The Krytos Trap (unabridged audiobook)}} *''[[X-Wing: The Bacta War]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: The Bacta War (unabridged audiobook)}} *''[[X-Wing: Wraith Squadron]]'' {{Ab|X-Wing: Wraith Squadron (unabridged audiobook)}} {{C|Indirect appearance, X-wing only}} *''[[X-Wing: Isard's Revenge]]'' *''[[I, Jedi]]'' {{Ab|I, Jedi (unabridged audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *''[[Fate of the Jedi: Outcast]]'' {{Ab|Fate of the Jedi: Outcast (audiobook)}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]'' *{{FactFile|14|ROG 1-2|X-Wing Rogue Squadron}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{StartBox}} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Rogue Seven/Legends|Rogue Seven]] |years=[[6 ABY/Legends|6.5 ABY]] |before=Eventually [[Feylis Ardele]] |after=[[Gavin Darklighter]] }} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Rogue Three/Legends|Rogue Three]] |years=[[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]] |before=[[Nawara Ven]] |after=[[Gavin Darklighter]] }} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Rogue Five/Legends|Rogue Five]] |years=[[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]] |before=[[Pash Cracken]] |after=Eventually [[Tal'dira]] }} {{EndBox}} {{Interlang |cs=Rhysati Ynr |de=Rhysati Ynr |hu=Rhysati Ynr |fi=Rhysati Ynr |pl=Rhysati Ynr }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ynr, Rhysati}} [[Category:Antilles's Rogues]] [[Category:Bespinites]] [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Rogue Squadron personnel]]
